s-1.ptxt: INSPECT VANE PUMP SHAFT AND BUSHING IN VANE PUMP FRONT HOUSING

s2.ptxt: Using a micrometer, measure the outer diameter [a] of the vane pump shaft with pulley.
Using a vernier caliper, measure the inner diameter [b] of the vane pump front housing bushing.
Oil clearance = Inner diameter of the bushing [b] - Outer diameter of the shaft [a].
If oil clearance exceeds the maximum, replace the vane pump assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT VANE PUMP ROTOR AND VANE PUMP PLATE CLEARANCE

s2.ptxt: Using a micrometer, measure the thickness of the vane pump plates.
If the thickness is not within the specified range, replace the vane pump assembly.
Using a feeler gauge, measure the clearance between the side face of the vane pump rotor groove and the vane pump plates.
If clearance exceeds the maximum, replace the vane pump assembly.
Coat the flow control valve assembly with power steering fluid and check that it falls smoothly into the flow control valve due to its own weight.
If the control valve does not fall into the hole smoothly, replace the vane pump assembly.

Check the flow control valve assembly for leakage. Close one of the holes and apply compressed air, 392 to 490 kPa (4 to 5 kgf/cm2, 57 to 71 psi), into the opposite side hole, and confirm that air does not come out from the end holes.
If air leaks, replace the vane pump assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT FLOW CONTROL VALVE COMPRESSION SPRING

s2.ptxt: Using a vernier caliper, measure the free length of the flow control valve compression spring.
If the length is less than the minimum, replace the vane pump assembly.

content1.ptxt: If the union seat in the pressure port union is severely damaged, replace the vane pump assembly.

s2.ptxt: Check that the pump rotates smoothly without abnormal noise.
Using a torque wrench, check the pump rotating torque.
If the rotating torque is not as specified, check the vane pump housing oil seal.
